---
status: draft
shortDescription: "Scheduling the execution of a function after 0 milliseconds with setTimeout is equivalent to a synchronous call"
shortCorrection: "Scheduling the execution of a function after 0 milliseconds with setTimeout is not equivalent to a synchronous call"
concepts:
  - Call
# TODO add async concept
related:
  # - id: LiteralNoExpression
  #   relation: Special case
inOtherPl:
  # - pl: Java
  #   id: NoAtomicExpression
  # - pl: Python
  #   id: NoAtomicExpression
spec:
  # - id: ecma11
  #   sections: ["11.8"]
plVersion:
  # from: ecma6
  # to: ecma11
---

Calling `setTimeout(f, 0)` is equivalent to a synchronous call of the function `f`.

frontmatter.shortDescription: Scheduling the execution of a function after 0 milliseconds with setTimeout is equivalent to a synchronous call
frontmatter.shortCorrection: Scheduling the execution of a function after 0 milliseconds with setTimeout is not equivalent to a synchronous call
